newsSince: Around IFAT 2026 / PRSE, TOMRA unveiled an AI-native PolyPerception platform (raising its stake to a 51% majority) with new deep-learning GAINnext applications, and launched the AI-based FINDER metals-sorting system able to sort PCBs/E-scrap and recover non-ferrous metals. These make the 'sensor sorting recovers metals from accelerating hardware cycles' linkage more concrete, though still early. No 2026 guidance change.

sources.label: TOMRA AI FINDER for metal recycling (AlCircle)

verdicts.rankRationale: The AI-native PolyPerception majority stake and new AI FINDER (PCB/E-scrap and non-ferrous recovery) make the previously 'speculative and unproven' AI-chain linkage more concrete, marginally improving the strict-screen case vs June 9. Mild positive only; still a circular-economy story, not a core compute bottleneck.
verdicts.bull: AI-native sorting (FINDER, GAINnext) directly targets E-scrap/PCB and metals recovery, tying TOMRA to the hardware-replacement cycle.
verdicts.neutral: Product launches strengthen the narrative but flow through revenue slowly; AI link to compute campuses remains indirect.
verdicts.bear: -30% YTD reflects weak end-markets; recycling demand tie to AI hardware cycles is still tenuous and unproven.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI sorting (FINDER/PolyPerception) for E-scrap & metals recovery
verdicts.primaryRisk: Weak end-market demand; AI-chain link remains indirect
